sentences.english: So, since we're going to be living together, maybe we should set some ground rules? Just so we're on the same page.
sentences.korean: 같이 살 거니까 기본 규칙을 좀 정할까? 서로 같은 생각이 되도록.

sentences.english: Yeah, that's a good idea. What were you thinking?
sentences.korean: 응, 좋은 생각이야. 뭘 생각하고 있었어?

sentences.english: Well, the biggest thing for me is cleanliness. I'm not a neat freak or anything, but I'd like us to keep the common areas — like the kitchen and the living room — reasonably tidy.
sentences.korean: 나한테 가장 중요한 건 청결이야. 결벽증은 아닌데, 공용 공간은 깔끔하게 유지했으면 해.

sentences.english: Totally agree. How about we take turns with the dishes? Like, I'll do them on weekdays and you do weekends? Or we could each just wash our own.
sentences.korean: 완전 동의. 설거지를 번갈아 하는 건 어때?

sentences.english: Let's each wash our own. That seems simpler. What about noise?
sentences.korean: 각자 자기 거 씻자. 더 간단하잖아. 소음은 어때?

quiz.question: What is the main topic of this passage?
options: History Lecture — The Fall of the Berlin Wall

sentences.english: Good point. I sometimes play guitar in the evenings. Is that going to be an issue?
sentences.korean: 좋은 지적이야. 저녁에 가끔 기타를 치는데, 문제가 될까?

sentences.english: Not at all, as long as it's not super late. Maybe keep it before 10 p.m. on weeknights?
sentences.korean: 전혀 괜찮아, 너무 늦지만 않으면 돼. 평일엔 밤 10시 전까지만 하면 될까?

sentences.english: That's fair. And I should mention — I have a friend who visits pretty often. He usually stays for dinner. Is that cool?
sentences.korean: 그거 공평해. 말해둘 게 있는데 — 꽤 자주 오는 친구가 있어. 보통 저녁까지 있거든. 괜찮아?

sentences.english: Of course. Just give me a heads-up so I know if I need to make extra food or whatever.
sentences.korean: 물론이지. 미리 알려주기만 해, 음식을 더 만들어야 하는지 알 수 있게.

sentences.english: Deal. This is going to work out great.

quiz.question: What is the main topic of this passage?
options: Long-Form Journalism — The Ethics of Gene Editing
